channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: President Samia Suluhu Hassan Leads Major Progress in Tanzania's Sports Sector and AFCON 2027 Preparations
item.description: Tanzania iko kwenye mabadiliko makubwa katika sekta ya michezo chini ya uongozi wa Rais Samia Suluhu Hassan. Serikali imeweka nguvu kubwa katika uwekezaji wa miundombinu, sera na motisha kwa wanariadh...

item.title: Indonesia to Restrict Social Media Access for Children Under 16
item.description: Indonesia, the world's fourth most populous country, will implement a ban on social media access for children under 16, starting March 28, 2026. The decision targets 'high-risk platforms' including Ti...

item.title: Mass Displacement in Beirut as Israeli Airstrikes Target Southern Suburbs
item.description: Beirut is witnessing one of its largest mass displacements in recent history as Israeli airstrikes hit the southern suburbs and border villages. Over 500,000 residents have been forced from their home...

item.title: Renewed Efforts Needed to Protect Karura Forest
item.description: Karura Forest in Nairobi has been a symbol of environmental activism since the days of Prof Wangari Maathai, who led brave Kenyans to stop its grab in the late 1990s. Today, we face similar threats as...

item.title: Panthers dominate Broncos in 26-0 victory as errors cost Brisbane
item.description: In a one-sided NRL clash at Suncorp Stadium, the Penrith Panthers delivered a commanding 26-0 win over the Brisbane Broncos. Dylan Edwards shone with 230 running metres, six tackle busts, and a try, w...

item.title: NBI issues subpoenas to ex-Marines and officials linked to alleged cash deliveries
item.description: The National Bureau of Investigation (NBI) has formally issued subpoenas to former lawmaker Mike Defensor, lawyer Levito Baligod, and 18 men, mostly ex-Marines, who alleged they delivered suitcases of...

item.title: 13 Repatriation Flights Bring Stranded Indian Travellers from Gulf to Mumbai Amid Middle East Conflict
item.description: On Friday, a total of 13 repatriation flights landed at Mumbai airport to bring back Indian travellers stranded in the Gulf due to ongoing tensions in the Middle East. Flights operated by SpiceJet, Em...

item.title: Multilateral Wheeling as a Pathway to Expanded Market Access for South African IPPs
item.description: South Africa’s electricity landscape is evolving rapidly, not just in terms of generation, but in how power is traded. Regulatory reforms have opened the national grid to private participation, signal...

item.title: Traditional Cowboy Sport Thrives in Venezuela
item.description: In Venezuela, a traditional sport known as coleo remains a popular and culturally significant activity. Coleo involves cowboys, called llaneros, chasing cattle on horseback and attempting to topple th...

item.title: The Wings Schools in Iwo Organise Farewell Ceremony for Pioneer Provost of Federal College of Education, Iwo
item.description: The management of The Wings Schools, Iwo, in Osun State, recently held a heartfelt send-off ceremony for Professor Abdulrafiu Adebayo, who has just completed his four-year tenure as the pioneer provos...

item.title: Tanzania’s Mining Sector Sees Fastest Credit Growth Among Industries in 2025
item.description: Dar es Salaam – Tanzania’s private sector continue to get more bank loans last year, with the mining and quarrying sector leading the growth. According to Bank of Tanzania’s Monthly Economic Review fo...

item.title: US-Iran Conflict Escalates Amid Calls for New Iranian Leadership
item.description: US President Donald Trump has demanded Iran’s unconditional surrender, overshadowing ongoing mediation efforts claimed by Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian. The remarks, posted on social media, sugg...

item.title: Warner Bros Plans Game of Thrones Film Exploring Aegon Targaryen's Conquest
item.description: Warner Bros is set to bring George R.R. Martin’s A Song of Ice and Fire universe to the big screen, focusing on Aegon Targaryen’s conquest of Westeros approximately 300 years before the events of the ...

item.title: UK Reviews Pakistani Asylum Cases Amid Concerns Over Low Deportation Rate
item.description: Recently, Pakistan has come under attention from the United Kingdom as concerns grow over the rising number of asylum applications from Pakistani nationals who initially enter Britain through legal vi...

item.title: Domestic LPG Price Increased and Nepal’s RSP Surges in Elections Amid Global Tensions
item.description: On March 7, 2026, domestic LPG prices in India saw a sharp increase of ₹60 per cylinder, with the new price for a non-subsidised 14.2-kg cylinder now at ₹913 in Delhi, as energy prices rise due to ong...

item.title: Metropolitan Police interview three women under caution in Mohamed al Fayed abuse facilitation probe
item.description: The Metropolitan Police have interviewed three women under caution as part of an ongoing investigation into allegations linked to the late Mohamed al Fayed, the former Harrods owner who died in 2023. ...

item.title: PRC Announces Results of February 2026 Nurses Licensure Exam
item.description: The Professional Regulation Commission (PRC) has officially released the results of the February 2026 Nurses Licensure Examination conducted by the Board of Nursing across 21 testing centers in the Ph...

item.title: Kenya Airways Evacuates Stranded Passengers From Middle East, Lands Safely in Nairobi
item.description: A Kenya Airways special repatriation flight safely touched down at Jomo Kenyatta International Airport, bringing back dozens of passengers stranded in the Middle East amid escalating tensions. The ope...

item.title: M-Pesa Reaches 40 Million Users as Mobile Finance Expands in Kenya
item.description: Safaricom's mobile money platform, M-Pesa, has seen significant growth, adding 6 million new users in 2026 to hit a total of 40 million monthly active users in Kenya. Since its launch on March 6, 2007...

item.title: The Evolution of Frankenstein's Bride as a Feminist Figure in Cinema
item.description: Frankenstein’s Bride, the female creature first seen on screen in James Whale’s 1935 film The Bride of Frankenstein, has become a lasting symbol of rebellion and feminist autonomy. Played memorably by...
